title: Quant-iT RiboGreen RNA Quantification Protocol
author: Roey Angel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfbxjipn
materials: Take out all reagents from the fridge and bring them to room temperature. Take out the RNA samples from the freezer. RNA samples should be slowly thawed on ice. Quant-iT邃｢ RiboGreenﾂｮ RNA reagent is dissolved in dimethylsulfoxide (DMSO), which freezes below 19 ﾂｰC. The reagent must be completely thawed before using it by bringing it to room temperature. After the reagent thawed, it is advisable to briefly vortex the tube to make sure it is adequately mixed and to spin it down in a centrifuge or a mini centrifuge. Quant-iT邃｢ RiboGreenﾂｮ RNA reagent is light sensitive and should be protected from light at all times. Quant-iT邃｢ RiboGreenﾂｮ RNA Assay Kit Thermo Scientific Catalog #R11490

steps.title: Prepare 22 ml 1X TE buffer by pipetting 1
steps.instruction: Prepare 22 ml 1X TE buffer by pipetting 1.1 ml of 20X TE buffer into 20.9 ml of nuclease-free water into a sterile and nuclease-free 50 ml tube. Mix by inverting the tube several times. undetermined, 20X TE buffer undetermined, nuclease-free water

steps.title: For high-range quantification
steps.instruction: For high-range quantification: Dilute the E.coli rRNA-standard stock solution (100 ﾎｼg ml -1 ) to a final concentration of 2 ng ﾎｼl -1 by mixing 10 ﾎｼl of rRNA-standard stock solution with 490 ﾎｼl 1X TE buffer. 10 ﾂｵL, rRNA-standard stock solution 490 ﾂｵL, 1X TE buffer For low-range quantification: Prepare a 20-fold dilution of the 2 ng ﾎｼl -1 rRNA-standard work solution by mixing 10 ﾎｼl of the 2 ng ﾎｼl -1 rRNA-standard work solution with 190 ﾎｼl 1X TE buffer to yield 0.1 ng ﾂｵl -1 rRNA-standard work solution. 10 ﾂｵL, diluted rRNA-standard solution 190 ﾂｵL, 1X TE buffer

steps.title: If needed, prepare a dilution of...
steps.instruction: If needed, prepare a dilution of each sample in 1X TE so that the reading will be within the dynamic range. It is advisable to run samples in duplicates for a more accurate quantification

steps.title: Prepare the RiboGreenﾂｮ work solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the RiboGreenﾂｮ work solution: For the high-range assay pipette 50 ﾎｼl Quant-iT邃｢ RiboGreenﾂｮ RNA Reagent and 9950 ﾂｵl of 1X TE into a sterile and nucleic-acids free 50 ml tube. For the low-range assay pipette 5 ﾎｼl Quant-iT邃｢ RiboGreenﾂｮ RNA Reagent and 9995 ﾂｵl of 1X TE into a sterile and nucleic-acids free 50 ml tube. Mix by inverting and keep the solution away from light.

steps.title: Prepare one of the following standard...
steps.instruction: Prepare one of the following standard mixtures in the first two columns of a black, sterile, 96-well plate: %s %s 96-Well microtiter plate (Polystyrene cell-culture plate, Nunc, 165305, Black, flat-bottom, sterile, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/152028#/152028 )

steps.title: Pipette 99 ﾂｵl of 1X TE...
steps.instruction: Pipette 99 ﾂｵl of 1X TE buffer in the remaining wells. undetermined, 1X TE buffer Tip: use a mechanical or electronic dispenser during this step and step no. 9 to speed up the work. Multipetteﾂｮ E3 (Electronic dispenser, eppendorf, 4987000010, http://multipette-system.eppendorf.com/ )

steps.title: Pipette 1 ﾂｵl of the unknown...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 ﾂｵl of the unknown RNA samples in the remaining wells. 1 ﾂｵL, Unknown RNA sample Use either a diluted sample in case the concentration is expected to be higher than the dynamic range limit or larger volume in case the concentration is expected to be below the detection limit.

steps.title: Pipette 100 ﾂｵl of the RiboGreenﾂｮ...
steps.instruction: Pipette 100 ﾂｵl of the RiboGreenﾂｮ work solution into each well, including the standard and unknown sample wells undetermined, RicoGreen work solution

steps.title: Protect the 96-well plate from light...
steps.instruction: Protect the 96-well plate from light and incubate for 2-5 min at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Place the plate in a plate...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a plate reader and measure the fluorescence according to the following parameters: Excitation ~480 nm Emission ~520 nm Integration time 40 s Lag time 0 s Gain Optimal Number of flashes 10 Calculated well highest standard Shaking 5 s Infinite M Nano (Absorbance plate reader, Tecan, TEC006436I, https://lifesciences.tecan.com/plate_readers/fluorescence_absorbance_luminescence?p=tab--2 ) It is also possible to set the gain to a fixed value (e.g. 100). If the fluorescence values of the standard drop over time this could indicate damage to the reagents or the RNA standard.

steps.title: Plot the measured fluorescent values of...
steps.instruction: Plot the measured fluorescent values of the standard samples against their known concentrations and fit a linear curve using linear regression. Make sure that the coefficient of determination (R 2 ) is close to 1 (typically >0.98). Calculate the RNA concentrations in the unknown samples using the slope and intercept parameters of the linear equation. Output values you obtained are in ng ﾂｵl -1 , assuming 1 ﾂｵl of each sample was used. Do not forget to account for any dilutions when calculating the concentration of the RNA in the unknown samples
